Title: NSA-2025-65-A_1
Description:
BHI supported PAHO in the scaling up the cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB):
BHI assisted in reviewing data to plan and conduct the scaling up of the program until full implementation as part of the MOH’s health care services. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force and other medical key personnel.
DirectResults:
The full implementation a national cervical cancer prevention program in AB.
•	Provision of a customized training delivered to medical staff and MOH)employees.
•	Planning and execution of a pilot HPV screening/treatment program w/ >1,500 women.
•	Based on learnings from pilot, scaled up of program to include another 3,000 women
•	Debriefed MOH about KPIs and key observation to optimize the full implementation.
History: emumenthaler@basichealth.org created a new activity on 12/22/2025 07:30:59 PM.
DescriptionENG: BHI supported PAHO in expanding cervical cancer screening efforts in Antigua and Barbuda (AB): BHI assisted in reviewing data to plan and implement the scaling up of the program until full implementation as part of the Ministry of Health's (MOH) healthcare services. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of the appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force, and other key medical personnel.
DescriptionSPA:
BHI apoyó a la OPS en la ampliación de los esfuerzos de detección del cáncer de cuello uterino en Antigua y Barbuda (AB):  
BHI asistió en la revisión de datos para planificar y llevar a cabo la ampliación del programa hasta su plena implementación como parte de los servicios de salud del Ministerio de Salud (MOH). BHI apoyó y colaboró directamente con el Ministerio de Salud (MOH) de AB, varios miembros del Grupo de Trabajo sobre Cáncer de Cuello Uterino designado y otros profesionales médicos clave.
DirectResultsENG:
The full implementation of a national cervical cancer prevention program in AB.
• Provision of customized training delivered to medical staff and Ministry of Health (MOH) employees.
• Planning and execution of a pilot HPV screening/treatment program with over 1,500 women.
• Based on learnings from the pilot, the program was scaled up to include an additional 3,000 women.
• Debriefed the MOH about key performance indicators (KPIs) and key observations to optimize the full implementation.
DirectResultsSPA:
La implementación completa de un programa nacional de prevención del cáncer de cuello uterino en AB.
• Provisión de una capacitación personalizada dirigida al personal médico y empleados del Ministerio de Salud (MOH).
• Planificación y ejecución de un programa piloto de detección/tratamiento de VPH con más de 1,500 mujeres.
• Basado en los aprendizajes del piloto, ampliación del programa para incluir a otras 3,000 mujeres.
• Informe al MOH sobre los KPIs y observaciones clave para optimizar la implementación completa.
DescriptionFRE:
BHI a soutenu l'OPS dans l'intensification des efforts de dépistage du cancer du col de l'utérus à Antigua-et-Barbuda (AB) :  
BHI a aidé à examiner les données pour planifier et réaliser l'intensification du programme jusqu'à sa pleine mise en œuvre dans le cadre des services de santé du ministère de la Santé (MOH). BHI a directement soutenu et collaboré avec le ministère de la Santé (MOH) d'AB, divers membres du groupe de travail sur le cancer du col de l'utérus et d'autres personnels médicaux clés.
DescriptionPOR:
O BHI apoiou a OPAS na ampliação dos esforços de triagem do câncer cervical em Antígua e Barbuda (AB):  
O BHI ajudou na revisão de dados para planejar e conduzir a ampliação do programa até a implementação total como parte dos serviços de saúde do Ministério da Saúde (MOH). O BHI apoiou diretamente e colaborou com o Ministério da Saúde (MOH) de AB, vários membros do Grupo de Trabalho sobre Câncer Cervical nomeado e outros profissionais médicos-chave.
DirectResultsFRE:
La mise en œuvre complète d'un programme national de prévention du cancer du col de l'utérus en AB.
• Fourniture d'une formation personnalisée dispensée au personnel médical et aux employés du ministère de la Santé.
• Planification et exécution d'un programme pilote de dépistage/traitement du VPH auprès de plus de 1 500 femmes.
• Sur la base des enseignements tirés du pilote, élargissement du programme pour inclure 3 000 femmes supplémentaires.
• Débriefing avec le ministère de la Santé sur les indicateurs clés de performance (KPI) et les observations clés pour optimiser la mise en œuvre complète.
DirectResultsPOR:
A implementação completa de um programa nacional de prevenção do câncer cervical em AB.
•	Fornecimento de um treinamento personalizado para a equipe médica e funcionários do Ministério da Saúde (MS).
•	Planejamento e execução de um programa piloto de triagem/tratamento de HPV com mais de 1.500 mulheres.
•	Baseado nas lições aprendidas com o piloto, ampliação do programa para incluir mais 3.000 mulheres.
•	Apresentação de um relatório ao MS sobre os KPIs e observações-chave para otimizar a implementação completa.

Title: NSA-2025-65-A_2
Description: BHI supported PAHO in the planning and rolling out of a national cervical cancer prevention program in St Vincent. BHI directly supported and collaborated with the Ministry of Health (MOH) of AB, various members of appointed Cervical Cancer Task Force and other medical key personnel.
DirectResults:
The implementation of a cervical cancer screening program in St Vincent until it is fully incorporated as part of country’s health care services.
•	BHI performed a needs assessment on the ground.
•	BHI created the database to be used by the MOH for the pilot and scale up phase so that quality data is available for analysis and decision making for the national rollout. 
•	Planned and conducted a custom-tailored training program covering anatomy, HPV specific screening/training and use of clinical equipment orientation.

Title: NSA-2025-62-A_1
Description: Support country implementation teams on social marketing campaigns through the provision of tutors, development of case studies, and funding; continue evaluation and monitoring efforts; and disseminate best practices.
DirectResults:
AHA continued its support to the virtual learning Program on Social Marketing for Public Health, launched in July 2022, by promoting modules on the PAHO Virtual Campus of Public Health across its networks.

More than 22,000 participants have engaged in the self-learning modality. In the tutorized modality, technical assistance and tutors supported national teams with formative field research and the implementation of social marketing components, resulting in fifteen projects prepared by country teams. 

A best practices webinar titled “Program on Social Marketing for Public Health: Best Practices” was held in August 2023, which featured several country teams. Aruba, Costa Rica, Panama, Puerto Rico, and Sint Maarten received support for sodium reduction campaigns. These experiences have been compiled into a guide for applying Social Marketing to the development of health interventions, which will be launched in the first quarter of 2026.
History: Sophia Nilanont created a new activity on 12/22/2025 07:49:57 PM.
DescriptionENG: Support country implementation teams on social marketing campaigns by providing tutors, developing case studies, and offering funding; continue evaluation and monitoring efforts; and share best practices.
DescriptionSPA: Apoyar a los equipos de implementación en los países en campañas de marketing social a través de la provisión de tutores, el desarrollo de estudios de caso y financiamiento; continuar con los esfuerzos de evaluación y monitoreo; y difundir las mejores prácticas.
DirectResultsENG:
AHA continued its support for the virtual learning Program on Social Marketing for Public Health, launched in July 2022, by promoting modules on the PAHO Virtual Campus of Public Health across its networks.

More than 22,000 participants have engaged in the self-learning modality. In the tutor-led modality, technical assistance and tutors supported national teams with formative field research and the implementation of social marketing components, resulting in fifteen projects prepared by country teams.

A best practices webinar titled “Program on Social Marketing for Public Health: Best Practices” was held in August 2023, featuring several country teams. Aruba, Costa Rica, Panama, Puerto Rico, and Sint Maarten received support for sodium reduction campaigns. These experiences have been compiled into a guide for applying Social Marketing to the development of health interventions, which will be launched in the first quarter of 2026.
DirectResultsSPA:
AHA continuó su apoyo al Programa de Aprendizaje Virtual sobre Marketing Social para la Salud Pública, lanzado en julio de 2022, promoviendo módulos en el Campus Virtual de Salud Pública de la OPS a través de sus redes.

Más de 22,000 participantes han participado en la modalidad de autoaprendizaje. En la modalidad tutorada, la asistencia técnica y los tutores apoyaron a los equipos nacionales con investigaciones de campo formativas y la implementación de componentes de marketing social, lo que resultó en quince proyectos preparados por los equipos de los países.

Se llevó a cabo un seminario web sobre mejores prácticas titulado “Programa de Marketing Social para la Salud Pública: Mejores Prácticas” en agosto de 2023, que contó con la participación de varios equipos de países. Aruba, Costa Rica, Panamá, Puerto Rico y Sint Maarten recibieron apoyo para campañas de reducción de sodio. Estas experiencias se han recopilado en una guía para aplicar el Marketing Social al desarrollo de intervenciones en salud, que se lanzará en el primer trimestre de 2026.
DescriptionFRE: Soutenir les équipes de mise en œuvre des pays sur les campagnes de marketing social par la fourniture de tuteurs, le développement d'études de cas et le financement ; continuer les efforts d'évaluation et de suivi ; et diffuser les meilleures pratiques.
DescriptionPOR: Apoiar as equipes de implementação do país em campanhas de marketing social por meio da provisão de tutores, desenvolvimento de estudos de caso e financiamento; continuar os esforços de avaliação e monitoramento; e disseminar as melhores práticas.
DirectResultsFRE:
AHA a poursuivi son soutien au programme d'apprentissage virtuel sur le marketing social pour la santé publique, lancé en juillet 2022, en promouvant des modules sur le Campus Virtuel de la Santé Publique de l'OPS à travers ses réseaux.

Plus de 22 000 participants se sont engagés dans la modalité d'auto-apprentissage. Dans la modalité tutorisée, une assistance technique et des tuteurs ont soutenu les équipes nationales avec des recherches de terrain formatives et la mise en œuvre de composants de marketing social, ce qui a abouti à la préparation de quinze projets par les équipes des pays.

Un webinaire sur les meilleures pratiques intitulé « Programme de marketing social pour la santé publique : Meilleures pratiques » a eu lieu en août 2023, mettant en avant plusieurs équipes nationales. Aruba, Costa Rica, Panama, Porto Rico et Sint Maarten ont reçu un soutien pour des campagnes de réduction de sodium. Ces expériences ont été compilées dans un guide pour appliquer le marketing social au développement d'interventions en santé, qui sera lancé au premier trimestre de 2026.
DirectResultsPOR:
A AHA continuou seu apoio ao Programa de Aprendizagem Virtual em Marketing Social para a Saúde Pública, lançado em julho de 2022, promovendo módulos no Campus Virtual de Saúde Pública da OPAS em suas redes.

Mais de 22.000 participantes se envolveram na modalidade de autoaprendizagem. Na modalidade tutorada, assistência técnica e tutores apoiaram equipes nacionais com pesquisa de campo formativa e a implementação de componentes de marketing social, resultando em quinze projetos elaborados pelas equipes dos países.

Um webinar de melhores práticas intitulado “Programa de Marketing Social para a Saúde Pública: Melhores Práticas” foi realizado em agosto de 2023, que contou com a participação de várias equipes de países. Aruba, Costa Rica, Panamá, Porto Rico e Sint Maarten receberam apoio para campanhas de redução de sódio. Essas experiências foram compiladas em um guia para a aplicação do Marketing Social no desenvolvimento de intervenções em saúde, que será lançado no primeiro trimestre de 2026.

Title: NSA-2025-62-A_2
Description: Collaborate to increase awareness on pregnancy risk factors associated with NCDs and develop collateral materials to support educational efforts.
DirectResults:
AHA has actively participated in its global coalitions to elevate women’s heart health and maternal health, primarily through the integration of the PAHO Zero Maternal Deaths initiative with the AHA-InterAmerican Society of Cardiology (IASC) joint maternal campaign, #CorazondeMama. 

Ongoing advocacy and educational work were conducted with IASC and the Inter-American Heart Foundation (IAHF), including constituency statements at the WHO and PAHO regional meetings on pandemic preparedness, universal health coverage, and adolescent and child heart conditions, such as congenital heart disease and rheumatic heart disease.
History: Sophia Nilanont created a new activity on 12/22/2025 07:49:57 PM.
DescriptionENG: Collaborate to raise awareness about pregnancy risk factors related to non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and create supporting materials for educational initiatives.
DescriptionSPA: Colaborar para aumentar la concienciación sobre los factores de riesgo de embarazo asociados con las enfermedades no transmisibles y desarrollar materiales colaterales para apoyar los esfuerzos educativos.
DirectResultsENG:
The AHA has actively participated in its global coalitions to promote women's heart health and maternal health, primarily through the integration of the PAHO Zero Maternal Deaths initiative with the AHA-InterAmerican Society of Cardiology (IASC) joint maternal campaign, #CorazondeMama.

Ongoing advocacy and educational efforts were carried out with IASC and the Inter-American Heart Foundation (IAHF), including constituency statements at the WHO and PAHO regional meetings on pandemic preparedness, universal health coverage, and heart conditions in adolescents and children, such as congenital heart disease and rheumatic heart disease.
DirectResultsSPA:
AHA ha participado activamente en sus coaliciones globales para elevar la salud cardíaca de las mujeres y la salud materna, principalmente a través de la integración de la iniciativa de la OPS Cero Muertes Maternas con la campaña conjunta de AHA y la Sociedad Interamericana de Cardiología (IASC), #CorazondeMama.

Se llevaron a cabo trabajos de defensa y educativos continuos con la IASC y la Fundación Interamericana del Corazón (IAHF), incluyendo declaraciones de los grupos de interés en las reuniones regionales de la OMS y la OPS sobre preparación para pandemias, cobertura de salud universal y condiciones cardíacas en adolescentes y niños, como las cardiopatías congénitas y la enfermedad cardíaca reumática.
DescriptionFRE: Collaborer pour sensibiliser aux facteurs de risque de grossesse associés aux maladies non transmissibles et développer des supports pour soutenir les efforts éducatifs.
DescriptionPOR: Colabore para aumentar a conscientização sobre os fatores de risco da gravidez associados às DCNTs e desenvolva materiais colaterais para apoiar os esforços educacionais.
DirectResultsFRE:
L'AHA a participé activement à ses coalitions mondiales pour promouvoir la santé cardiaque des femmes et la santé maternelle, principalement par l'intégration de l'initiative Zéro Décès Maternels de l'OPS avec la campagne conjointe de l'AHA et de la Société Interaméricaine de Cardiologie (IASC), #CorazondeMama.

Un travail de plaidoyer et d'éducation continu a été réalisé avec l'IASC et la Fondation Interaméricaine du Cœur (IAHF), y compris des déclarations de la base lors des réunions régionales de l'OMS et de l'OPS sur la préparation aux pandémies, la couverture santé universelle et les conditions cardiaques des adolescents et des enfants, telles que les cardiopathies congénitales et les cardiopathies rhumatismales.
DirectResultsPOR:
A AHA tem participado ativamente de suas coalizões globais para elevar a saúde cardíaca das mulheres e a saúde materna, principalmente por meio da integração da iniciativa Zero Mortes Maternas da OPAS com a campanha conjunta da AHA e da Sociedade Interamericana de Cardiologia (IASC), #CorazondeMama.

Trabalhos contínuos de advocacy e educação foram realizados com a IASC e a Fundação Interamericana do Coração (IAHF), incluindo declarações de constituintes nas reuniões regionais da OMS e da OPAS sobre preparação para pandemias, cobertura universal de saúde e condições cardíacas em adolescentes e crianças, como doenças cardíacas congênitas e doença cardíaca reumática.

Title: NSA-2025-62-A_3
Description: Build capacity around NCD risk factors to create health-promoting environments within the school setting, with engagement of Ministries of Health and Ministries of Education
DirectResults:
AHA has also supported the implementation of the PAHO/OAS Hemispheric Program on Healthy Eating Policies and Physical Activity in School Environments: “A first line of action on fighting noncommunicable diseases in the Americas”. Following a call for project proposals to create school environments conductive to healthy eating, nutrition, and physical activity, projects from ministries of education and health of the Bahamas, Belize, and Colombia were selected to receive intensified technical assistance.

The documentation of the experience and lessons learned are being integrated into a regional guidance document to be presented to the ministers of education of the Region.
History: Sophia Nilanont created a new activity on 12/22/2025 07:49:57 PM.
DescriptionENG: Build capacity around non-communicable disease (NCD) risk factors to create health-promoting environments within the school setting, with the involvement of Ministries of Health and Ministries of Education.
DescriptionSPA: Desarrollar capacidades en torno a los factores de riesgo de las enfermedades no transmisibles para crear entornos que promuevan la salud dentro del ámbito escolar, con la participación de los Ministerios de Salud y los Ministerios de Educación.
DirectResultsENG:
AHA has also supported the implementation of the PAHO/OAS Hemispheric Program on Healthy Eating Policies and Physical Activity in School Environments: “A first line of action on fighting noncommunicable diseases in the Americas.” Following a call for project proposals to create school environments conducive to healthy eating, nutrition, and physical activity, projects from the ministries of education and health of the Bahamas, Belize, and Colombia were selected to receive intensified technical assistance.

The documentation of the experience and lessons learned is being integrated into a regional guidance document to be presented to the ministers of education of the Region.
DirectResultsSPA:
AHA también ha apoyado la implementación del Programa Hemisférico de la OPS/OEA sobre Políticas de Alimentación Saludable y Actividad Física en Entornos Escolares: “Una primera línea de acción en la lucha contra las enfermedades no transmisibles en las Américas”. Tras una convocatoria para propuestas de proyectos para crear entornos escolares propicios para la alimentación saludable, la nutrición y la actividad física, se seleccionaron proyectos de los ministerios de educación y salud de las Bahamas, Belice y Colombia para recibir asistencia técnica intensificada.

La documentación de la experiencia y las lecciones aprendidas se está integrando en un documento de orientación regional que será presentado a los ministros de educación de la Región.
DescriptionFRE: Renforcer les capacités autour des facteurs de risque des maladies non transmissibles pour créer des environnements favorables à la santé au sein du cadre scolaire, avec l'engagement des ministères de la Santé et des ministères de l'Éducation.
DescriptionPOR: Desenvolver capacidade em torno dos fatores de risco das DCNT para criar ambientes que promovam a saúde no contexto escolar, com o envolvimento dos Ministérios da Saúde e dos Ministérios da Educação.
DirectResultsFRE:
L'AHA a également soutenu la mise en œuvre du Programme hémisphérique de l'OPS/OEA sur les politiques de nutrition saine et l'activité physique dans les environnements scolaires : « Une première ligne d'action pour lutter contre les maladies non transmissibles dans les Amériques ». Suite à un appel à propositions de projets visant à créer des environnements scolaires propices à une alimentation saine, à la nutrition et à l'activité physique, des projets des ministères de l'éducation et de la santé des Bahamas, du Belize et de la Colombie ont été sélectionnés pour recevoir une assistance technique renforcée.

La documentation de l'expérience et des leçons apprises est en cours d'intégration dans un document d'orientation régional qui sera présenté aux ministres de l'éducation de la région.
DirectResultsPOR:
A AHA também apoiou a implementação do Programa Hemisférico da OPAS/OEA sobre Políticas de Alimentação Saudável e Atividade Física em Ambientes Escolares: “Uma linha de ação prioritária no combate às doenças não transmissíveis nas Américas”. Após um chamado para propostas de projetos para criar ambientes escolares propícios à alimentação saudável, nutrição e atividade física, projetos dos ministérios da educação e da saúde das Bahamas, Belize e Colômbia foram selecionados para receber assistência técnica intensificada.

A documentação da experiência e das lições aprendidas está sendo integrada em um documento de orientação regional a ser apresentado aos ministros da educação da Região.

Title: NSA-2025-18-A_1
Description: Baseline Data and Air Quality Management: Generated and synthesized baseline data to identify opportunities for improvement and strengthen air quality management tools, and support the development of integrated plans.
DirectResults:
-Air quality management capacities in 152 cities across Latin America and the Caribbean were mapped and visualized to effectively communicate key findings.
-This comprehensive dataset supported several initiatives, including the development of background analysis for updating Trinidad and Tobago’s National Air Pollution Rules.
-The dataset also served as a foundational input for updating the Regional Air Quality Action Plan for Latin America and the Caribbean (2022–2025) of the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP).
History: Stephanie Montero created a new activity on 12/22/2025 08:56:04 PM.
DescriptionENG: Baseline Data and Air Quality Management: Generated and synthesized baseline data to identify opportunities for improvement and strengthen air quality management tools, and support the development of integrated plans.
DescriptionSPA: Datos de referencia y gestión de la calidad del aire: Se generaron y sintetizaron datos de referencia para identificar oportunidades de mejora y fortalecer las herramientas de gestión de la calidad del aire, así como apoyar el desarrollo de planes integrados.
DirectResultsENG:
- Air quality management capacities in 152 cities across Latin America and the Caribbean were mapped and visualized to effectively communicate key findings.
- This comprehensive dataset supported several initiatives, including the development of background analysis for updating Trinidad and Tobago’s National Air Pollution Rules.
- The dataset also served as a foundational input for updating the Regional Air Quality Action Plan for Latin America and the Caribbean (2022–2025) of the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP).
DirectResultsSPA:
- Se mapearon y visualizaron las capacidades de gestión de la calidad del aire en 152 ciudades de América Latina y el Caribe para comunicar de manera efectiva los hallazgos clave.  
- Este conjunto de datos integral apoyó varias iniciativas, incluida la elaboración de un análisis de antecedentes para actualizar las Normas Nacionales de Contaminación del Aire de Trinidad y Tobago.  
- El conjunto de datos también sirvió como un insumo fundamental para actualizar el Plan de Acción Regional de Calidad del Aire para América Latina y el Caribe (2022–2025) del Programa de las Naciones Unidas para el Medio Ambiente (PNUMA).

DirectResultsFRE:

Title: NSA-2025-18-A_2
Description: Capacity Building: Organized joint capacity-building initiatives, such as webinars, workshops, and training resources covering air quality management, monitoring, emission inventories, and health risk assessments. Key PAHO and WHO tools leveraged include: AirQ+: Software Tool for Health Risk Assessment of Air Pollution, WHO Household Energy Assessment Rapid Tool (HEART) and Carbon Reduction Benefits on Health (CaRBonH) Calculation Tool.
DirectResults:
-Supported the implementation of the WHO Household Energy Assessment Rapid Tool in Honduras, Panama, Peru, and Paraguay to enhance data-driven decision-making.
-Updated PAHO Household Air Pollution Country Profiles in Belize, Bolivia, Colombia, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, and Peru to inform policy-making.
-Strengthened the health sector’s capacity in Colombia to evaluate health co-benefits of climate change and air quality mitigation actions using AirQ+. Professionals from 20 cities were trained.

DescriptionFRE: Renforcement des capacités : Initiatives conjointes de renforcement des capacités organisées, telles que des webinaires, des ateliers et des ressources de formation couvrant la gestion de la qualité de l'air, la surveillance, les inventaires d'émissions et les évaluations des risques pour la santé. DescriptionPOR: Desenvolvimento de Capacidades: Organizou iniciativas conjuntas de desenvolvimento de capacidades, como webinars, oficinas e recursos de treinamento abordando gestão da qualidade do ar, monitoramento, inventários de emissões e avaliações de risco à saúde. As principais ferramentas da OPAS e da OMS utilizadas incluem: AirQ+: Ferramenta de Software para Avaliação de Risco à Saúde da Poluição do Ar, Ferramenta Rápida de Avaliação de Energia Doméstica da OMS (HEART) e Ferramenta de Cálculo dos Benefícios da Redução de Carbono para a Saúde (CaRBonH).
DirectResultsFRE:
- A soutenu la mise en œuvre de l'outil d'évaluation rapide de l'énergie domestique de l'OMS au Honduras, au Panama, au Pérou et au Paraguay pour améliorer la prise de décision basée sur les données.
- A mis à jour les profils nationaux de pollution de l'air domestique de l'OPS au Belize, en Bolivie, en Colombie, au Guatemala, au Honduras, au Mexique, au Nicaragua, au Panama, au Paraguay et au Pérou pour éclairer l'élaboration des politiques.
- A renforcé la capacité du secteur de la santé en Colombie à évaluer les co-bénéfices pour la santé des actions d'atténuation du changement climatique et de la qualité de l'air en utilisant AirQ+. Des professionnels de 20 villes ont été formés.
- A réalisé une formation en personne sur la surveillance de la qualité de l'air (du 12 au 14 mars 2024) et a dispensé un cours virtuel guidé (de mai à septembre 2024) intitulé « Gestion complète de la qualité de l'air et de la santé dans le cadre de l'action climatique : Guide pour les décideurs en Amérique centrale et en République dominicaine. » Plus de 40 participants des secteurs de la santé et de l'environnement ont participé, définissant des actions prioritaires et estimant les bénéfices pour la santé.
DirectResultsPOR:
- Apoiei a implementação da Ferramenta Rápida de Avaliação de Energia Doméstica da OMS em Honduras, Panamá, Peru e Paraguai para aprimorar a tomada de decisões baseada em dados.
- Atualizei os Perfis de Poluição do Ar Doméstico da OPAS em Belize, Bolívia, Colômbia, Guatemala, Honduras, México, Nicarágua, Panamá, Paraguai e Peru para informar a formulação de políticas.
- Fortaleci a capacidade do setor de saúde na Colômbia para avaliar os co-benefícios à saúde das ações de mitigação das mudanças climáticas e da qualidade do ar utilizando o AirQ+. Profissionais de 20 cidades foram treinados.
- Realizei um treinamento presencial sobre monitoramento da qualidade do ar (de 12 a 14 de março de 2024) e ofereci um curso virtual guiado (de maio a setembro de 2024) intitulado “Gestão Abrangente da Qualidade do Ar e Saúde no Âmbito da Ação Climática: Orientações para Tomadores de Decisão na América Central e na República Dominicana.” Mais de 40 participantes dos setores de saúde e meio ambiente participaram, definindo ações prioritárias e estimando os benefícios à saúde.

Title: NSA-2025-18-A_4
Description: Regulatory Development and Updates: Supported the revision of national air quality standards in alignment with WHO guidelines through regulatory assessments, legislative guidance, emission reduction scenarios, air quality modeling, and health benefit evaluations using the AirQ+ tool.

DirectResultsFRE:

Title: NSA-2025-18-A_6
Description: Strengthened Air Quality Monitoring: Improved air quality monitoring through detailed assessments, tailored recommendations, the development of national and regional strategies, and the design of a prototype air quality monitoring station.
DirectResults:
-Developed a protocol for the design and operation of air quality monitoring in the Dominican Republic, along with a proposal for the redesign of the national monitoring network.
- Implemented an air quality and short-lived climate pollutant (SLCP) monitoring network assessment for countries of the Central American Integration System (SICA) region. This included one-on-one interviews, the development of a regional strategy for air quality monitoring, and the design of a prototype air pollution and hydro-meteorological monitoring system. These elements were discussed and validated by SICA member states during an in-person training workshop that included a site visit.

Title: NSA-2025-53-A_3
Description: Provide technical assistance to national cancer control planning efforts in selected countries in the Region, and guidance on developing clinical operations and patient navigation systems to enhance diagnosis and treatment of cancer patients.
DirectResults: In collaboration with the Belize Ministry of Health, the Social Security Board, and the KHMH Oncology Unit, the following gaps have been identified: (1) access to radiation treatments; (2) pediatric oncology care; and (3) country‑wide screening services for common cancers (including breast and cervical). Global Oncology has engaged local stakeholders to assess needs and advised on strategic plans for each area; updates and requests for additional work from national stakeholders are pending. GO has shared with PAHO and MoH the framework for the hospital‑based cancer navigation system implemented by GO and KHMH and offered to collaborate on providing hospital cancer case data.
History: Nicole Kim created a new activity on 12/23/2025 11:36:24 AM.
DescriptionENG: Provide technical assistance to national cancer control planning efforts in selected countries in the Region, and guidance on developing clinical operations and patient navigation systems to enhance the diagnosis and treatment of cancer patients.
DescriptionSPA: Proporcionar asistencia técnica a los esfuerzos de planificación del control del cáncer en países seleccionados de la región, y orientación sobre el desarrollo de operaciones clínicas y sistemas de navegación para pacientes para mejorar el diagnóstico y tratamiento de pacientes con cáncer.
DirectResultsENG: In collaboration with the Belize Ministry of Health, the Social Security Board, and the KHMH Oncology Unit, the following gaps have been identified: (1) access to radiation treatments; (2) pediatric oncology care; and (3) country-wide screening services for common cancers (including breast and cervical). Global Oncology has engaged local stakeholders to assess needs and advised on strategic plans for each area; updates and requests for additional work from national stakeholders are pending. GO has shared with PAHO and MoH the framework for the hospital-based cancer navigation system implemented by GO and KHMH and offered to collaborate on providing hospital cancer case data.
DirectResultsSPA: En colaboración con el Ministerio de Salud de Belice, la Junta de Seguridad Social y la Unidad de Oncología del KHMH, se han identificado las siguientes brechas: (1) acceso a tratamientos de radiación; (2) atención oncológica pediátrica; y (3) servicios de detección a nivel nacional para cánceres comunes (incluyendo cáncer de mama y cervical). Global Oncology ha involucrado a las partes interesadas locales para evaluar las necesidades y ha asesorado sobre planes estratégicos para cada área; se están esperando actualizaciones y solicitudes de trabajo adicional por parte de las partes interesadas nacionales. GO ha compartido con la OPS y el Ministerio de Salud el marco para el sistema de navegación de cáncer basado en hospitales implementado por GO y KHMH y se ha ofrecido a colaborar en la provisión de datos de casos de cáncer hospitalarios.
DescriptionFRE: Fournir une assistance technique aux efforts de planification du contrôle du cancer dans des pays sélectionnés de la région, ainsi que des conseils sur le développement des opérations cliniques et des systèmes de navigation des patients pour améliorer le diagnostic et le traitement des patients atteints de cancer.
DescriptionPOR: Fornecer assistência técnica aos esforços de planejamento nacional de controle do câncer em países selecionados da Região, e orientação sobre o desenvolvimento de operações clínicas e sistemas de navegação de pacientes para aprimorar o diagnóstico e tratamento de pacientes com câncer.
DirectResultsFRE: En collaboration avec le ministère de la Santé du Belize, le Conseil de sécurité sociale et l'unité d'oncologie de KHMH, les lacunes suivantes ont été identifiées : (1) l'accès aux traitements par radiothérapie ; (2) les soins d'oncologie pédiatrique ; et (3) les services de dépistage à l'échelle nationale pour les cancers courants (y compris le cancer du sein et le cancer du col de l'utérus). Global Oncology a engagé des parties prenantes locales pour évaluer les besoins et a conseillé sur des plans stratégiques pour chaque domaine ; des mises à jour et des demandes de travaux supplémentaires de la part des parties prenantes nationales sont en attente. GO a partagé avec l'OPS et le ministère de la Santé le cadre du système de navigation pour le cancer basé à l'hôpital mis en œuvre par GO et KHMH et a proposé de collaborer à la fourniture de données sur les cas de cancer à l'hôpital.
DirectResultsPOR: Em colaboração com o Ministério da Saúde de Belize, o Conselho de Segurança Social e a Unidade de Oncologia do KHMH, foram identificadas as seguintes lacunas: (1) acesso a tratamentos de radiação; (2) atendimento em oncologia pediátrica; e (3) serviços de triagem em todo o país para cânceres comuns (incluindo mama e colo do útero). A Global Oncology envolveu partes interessadas locais para avaliar as necessidades e aconselhou sobre planos estratégicos para cada área; atualizações e solicitações de trabalho adicional por parte das partes interessadas nacionais estão pendentes. A GO compartilhou com a OPAS e o Ministério da Saúde o framework para o sistema de navegação de câncer baseado em hospitais implementado pela GO e KHMH e ofereceu colaborar na fornecimento de dados de casos de câncer hospitalares.

Title: NSA-2025-24-A_1
Description:
Title of activity: Strengthen civil society capacity to promote effective policies for preventing non-communicable diseases or NCDs.
Description:  Strengthen the region’s civil society capacity to promote the adoption, implementation, and monitoring of effective policies to reduce risk factors and contribute to the prevention of chronic, non-communicable diseases.
DirectResults:
-3 of 4 Central American countries, jointly with PAHO, now have legislative proposals on Front-of-Package Warning Labels. Workshops held in Costa Rica (2023), Guatemala (2024) and Panama (2025), plus capacity-building with legislators.
-Bolivia, Ecuador and Panama, in collaboration with PAHO, advanced proposals to eliminate industrially produced trans fatty acids (TFA), engaging civil society to keep bills aligned with PAHO best practices.
-Advocacy for mandatory sodium reduction targets in Costa Rica and Panama, jointly with PAHO, through social mobilization, campaigns and events with civil society, academia and health authorities.
-With Colansa and PAHO, provided financial and technical support to 4 subgrants on agroecology, school environments, UPF marketing regulation and coalition building.
-Organized ALASA, a regional alliance to reduce alcohol consumption, supporting PAHO; now assisting Uruguay and Costa Rica to form national alliances.
History: Beatriz Champagne created a new activity on 12/23/2025 11:41:55 AM.
DescriptionENG:
**Title of activity:** Strengthen civil society capacity to promote effective policies for preventing non-communicable diseases (NCDs).  
**Description:** Strengthen the region’s civil society capacity to promote the adoption, implementation, and monitoring of effective policies to reduce risk factors and contribute to the prevention of chronic, non-communicable diseases.
DescriptionSPA:
Título de la actividad: Fortalecer la capacidad de la sociedad civil para promover políticas efectivas para la prevención de enfermedades no transmisibles o ENT.  
Descripción: Fortalecer la capacidad de la sociedad civil de la región para promover la adopción, implementación y monitoreo de políticas efectivas que reduzcan los factores de riesgo y contribuyan a la prevención de enfermedades crónicas no transmisibles.
DirectResultsENG:
- 3 out of 4 Central American countries, in collaboration with PAHO, now have legislative proposals for Front-of-Package Warning Labels. Workshops were held in Costa Rica (2023), Guatemala (2024), and Panama (2025), along with capacity-building efforts for legislators.
- Bolivia, Ecuador, and Panama, in partnership with PAHO, have advanced proposals to eliminate industrially produced trans fatty acids (TFA), engaging civil society to ensure that the bills align with PAHO's best practices.
- Advocacy for mandatory sodium reduction targets is underway in Costa Rica and Panama, in collaboration with PAHO, through social mobilization, campaigns, and events involving civil society, academia, and health authorities.
- Together with Colansa and PAHO, financial and technical support was provided for 4 subgrants focused on agroecology, school environments, UPF marketing regulation, and coalition building.
- ALASA, a regional alliance aimed at reducing alcohol consumption, was organized to support PAHO; it is now assisting Uruguay and Costa Rica in forming national alliances.
DirectResultsSPA:
- 3 de 4 países de América Central, en conjunto con la OPS, ahora tienen propuestas legislativas sobre Etiquetas de Advertencia en el Frente del Paquete. Se llevaron a cabo talleres en Costa Rica (2023), Guatemala (2024) y Panamá (2025), además de capacitación con legisladores.
- Bolivia, Ecuador y Panamá, en colaboración con la OPS, avanzaron propuestas para eliminar los ácidos grasos trans (TFA) producidos industrialmente, involucrando a la sociedad civil para mantener los proyectos de ley alineados con las mejores prácticas de la OPS.
- Abogacía por objetivos obligatorios de reducción de sodio en Costa Rica y Panamá, en conjunto con la OPS, a través de movilización social, campañas y eventos con la sociedad civil, la academia y las autoridades de salud.
- Con Colansa y la OPS, se proporcionó apoyo financiero y técnico a 4 subgrupos sobre agroecología, entornos escolares, regulación de la comercialización de alimentos ultraprocesados y construcción de coaliciones.
- Se organizó ALASA, una alianza regional para reducir el consumo de alcohol, apoyando a la OPS; ahora asistiendo a Uruguay y Costa Rica para formar alianzas nacionales.
DescriptionFRE:
Titre de l'activité : Renforcer la capacité de la société civile à promouvoir des politiques efficaces pour la prévention des maladies non transmissibles (MNT).  
Description : Renforcer la capacité de la société civile de la région à promouvoir l'adoption, la mise en œuvre et le suivi de politiques efficaces visant à réduire les facteurs de risque et à contribuer à la prévention des maladies chroniques non transmissibles.
DescriptionPOR:
Título da atividade: Fortalecer a capacidade da sociedade civil para promover políticas eficazes de prevenção de doenças não transmissíveis ou DNTs.  
Descrição: Fortalecer a capacidade da sociedade civil da região para promover a adoção, implementação e monitoramento de políticas eficazes para reduzir fatores de risco e contribuir para a prevenção de doenças crônicas não transmissíveis.
DirectResultsFRE:
- 3 des 4 pays d'Amérique centrale, en collaboration avec l'OPS, ont désormais des propositions législatives sur les étiquettes d'avertissement en avant de l'emballage. Des ateliers ont été organisés au Costa Rica (2023), au Guatemala (2024) et au Panama (2025), ainsi que des actions de renforcement des capacités avec les législateurs.
- La Bolivie, l'Équateur et le Panama, en collaboration avec l'OPS, ont avancé des propositions pour éliminer les acides gras trans (AGT) produits industriellement, en engageant la société civile pour maintenir les projets de loi alignés sur les meilleures pratiques de l'OPS.
- Plaidoyer pour des objectifs de réduction obligatoire du sodium au Costa Rica et au Panama, en collaboration avec l'OPS, à travers la mobilisation sociale, des campagnes et des événements avec la société civile, le milieu académique et les autorités sanitaires.
- Avec Colansa et l'OPS, soutien financier et technique à 4 sous-subventions sur l'agroécologie, les environnements scolaires, la réglementation du marketing des aliments ultra-transformés et la création de coalitions.
- Organisation d'ALASA, une alliance régionale pour réduire la consommation d'alcool, soutenant l'OPS ; maintenant, assistance à l'Uruguay et au Costa Rica pour former des alliances nationales.
DirectResultsPOR:
- 3 dos 4 países da América Central, em conjunto com a OPAS, agora têm propostas legislativas sobre Rótulos de Advertência na Frente da Embalagem. Oficinas realizadas na Costa Rica (2023), Guatemala (2024) e Panamá (2025), além de capacitação com legisladores.
- Bolívia, Equador e Panamá, em colaboração com a OPAS, avançaram propostas para eliminar os ácidos graxos trans (TFA) produzidos industrialmente, envolvendo a sociedade civil para manter os projetos de lei alinhados com as melhores práticas da OPAS.
- Advocacia por metas obrigatórias de redução de sódio na Costa Rica e no Panamá, em conjunto com a OPAS, por meio de mobilização social, campanhas e eventos com a sociedade civil, academia e autoridades de saúde.
- Com a Colansa e a OPAS, forneceu apoio financeiro e técnico a 4 subgrupos sobre agroecologia, ambientes escolares, regulação de marketing de UPF e construção de coalizões.
- Organizou a ALASA, uma aliança regional para reduzir o consumo de álcool, apoiando a OPAS; agora auxiliando o Uruguai e a Costa Rica na formação de alianças nacionais.

Title: NSA-2025-24-A_2
Description:
Title of activity:  Stronger communication for advocacy

Description: Convene communication experts from civil society and partner organizations and promote improved communication by connecting local, national, and regional levels to support advancing policy change. CLAS plans to organize a regional communication space that creates synergies, empowers local advocates, and articulates the efforts of various groups.
DirectResults:
-CLAS and Colansa launched SaludableSaberlo and, in collaboration with PAHO, expanded and integrated it with PAHO’s platforms to improve the reach of evidence-based information to the public, media, and decision-makers.
-CLAS, COLANSA, and PAHO jointly developed scientific materials and co-designed campaigns disseminated through their channels, integrating national and regional communications and social/traditional media to empower local advocates.
-With PAHO, SaludableSaberlo hosted webinars on journalism, conflict of interest, and digital marketing, and compiles top-read articles in a monthly newsletter.
-In Sept 2025, SaludableSaberlo and PAHO held a journalist workshop with 40+ participants from 10 countries. Reach has grown significantly since its Jan 2024 launch.

-CLAS y Colansa lanzaron SaludableSaberlo y, en colaboración con la OPS, lo expandieron e integraron con las plataformas de la OPS para mejorar el alcance de la información basada en evidencia al público, los medios y los tomadores de decisiones.

DescriptionFRE:

Title: NSA-2025-35-A_3
Description:
MOD co-leaded the organization of the 10th International Conference on Birth Defects focused on the theme: Accelerating Action for Birth Defects and Disabilities: Surveillance, Prevention, Screening, Management and Family-Centered Care was held Chile in 2023 March 1-4, 2023. 
MOD, along with PAHO, WHO, CDC, scientific and academic institutions as the International Clearinghouse for Birth Defects Surveillance and Research, co-organized and leaded the Conference.
DirectResults:
The conference had 220 registered participants with 189 in attendance. Participants included representation from 25 countries including (in alphabetical order) Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bolivia, Botswana, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, England, India, Kenya, Malawi, Namibia, Nepal, Nicaragua, Pakistan, Paraguay, Philippines, South Africa, Sri Lanka, Switzerland, Uganda, United States, and Uruguay.
Over the four-day conference, there were six plenary sessions and 114 presentations covering screening, surveillance, prevention, management and care, and parent engagement. There were also 84 posters featuring a range of topics and findings. A pre-conference workshop focused on surveillance was led by CDC and WHO with 32 participants.

As a next step from ICBD2023, participants aligned on initiating a Call to Action regarding the integration, prevention, screening, diagnosis, and management of birth defects and disability into routine health services.
History: Ernst, Kelly created a new activity on 12/23/2025 11:58:06 AM.
DescriptionENG: MOD co-led the organization of the 10th International Conference on Birth Defects, which focused on the theme: Accelerating Action for Birth Defects and Disabilities: Surveillance, Prevention, Screening, Management, and Family-Centered Care. The conference was held in Chile from March 1-4, 2023. MOD, along with PAHO, WHO, CDC, and scientific and academic institutions such as the International Clearinghouse for Birth Defects Surveillance and Research, co-organized and led the conference.
DescriptionSPA: MOD co-lideró la organización de la 10ª Conferencia Internacional sobre Defectos de Nacimiento, centrada en el tema: Acelerando la Acción para los Defectos de Nacimiento y Discapacidades: Vigilancia, Prevención, Detección, Manejo y Atención Centrada en la Familia, que se llevó a cabo en Chile del 1 al 4 de marzo de 2023. MOD, junto con la OPS, la OMS, los CDC y las instituciones científicas y académicas como el Centro Internacional de Información sobre Vigilancia e Investigación de Defectos de Nacimiento, co-organizó y lideró la Conferencia.
DirectResultsENG:
The conference had 220 registered participants, with 189 in attendance. Participants included representatives from 25 countries, listed in alphabetical order: Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bolivia, Botswana, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, England, India, Kenya, Malawi, Namibia, Nepal, Nicaragua, Pakistan, Paraguay, Philippines, South Africa, Sri Lanka, Switzerland, Uganda, the United States, and Uruguay. 

Over the four-day conference, there were six plenary sessions and 114 presentations covering topics such as screening, surveillance, prevention, management and care, and parent engagement. Additionally, there were 84 posters showcasing a variety of topics and findings. A pre-conference workshop focused on surveillance was conducted by the CDC and WHO, with 32 participants.

As a next step from ICBD2023, participants agreed to initiate a Call to Action regarding the integration, prevention, screening, diagnosis, and management of birth defects and disabilities into routine health services.
DirectResultsSPA:
La conferencia tuvo 220 participantes registrados, de los cuales 189 asistieron. Los participantes incluyeron representación de 25 países, incluyendo (en orden alfabético) Argentina, Australia, Bangladés, Bolivia, Botsuana, Brasil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Inglaterra, India, Kenia, Malaui, Namibia, Nepal, Nicaragua, Pakistán, Paraguay, Filipinas, Sudáfrica, Sri Lanka, Suiza, Uganda, Estados Unidos y Uruguay. 

Durante la conferencia de cuatro días, se llevaron a cabo seis sesiones plenarias y 114 presentaciones que abarcaron temas de detección, vigilancia, prevención, manejo y atención, así como la participación de los padres. También hubo 84 carteles que presentaron una variedad de temas y hallazgos. Un taller previo a la conferencia centrado en la vigilancia fue dirigido por los CDC y la OMS con 32 participantes.

Como siguiente paso de ICBD2023, los participantes se alinearon para iniciar un Llamado a la Acción respecto a la integración, prevención, detección, diagnóstico y manejo de los defectos de nacimiento y la discapacidad en los servicios de salud rutinarios.
DescriptionFRE: MOD a co-dirigé l'organisation de la 10e Conférence internationale sur les malformations congénitales, axée sur le thème : Accélérer l'action pour les malformations congénitales et les handicaps : Surveillance, Prévention, Dépistage, Gestion et Soins centrés sur la famille, qui s'est tenue au Chili du 1er au 4 mars 2023. MOD, en collaboration avec l'OPS, l'OMS, les CDC, ainsi que des institutions scientifiques et académiques telles que le Bureau international de surveillance et de recherche sur les malformations congénitales, a co-organisé et dirigé la Conférence.
DescriptionPOR: A MOD co-liderou a organização da 10ª Conferência Internacional sobre Defeitos Congênitos, com foco no tema: Acelerando Ações para Defeitos Congênitos e Deficiências: Vigilância, Prevenção, Triagem, Gestão e Cuidados Centrado na Família, que foi realizada no Chile de 1 a 4 de março de 2023. A MOD, juntamente com a OPAS, OMS, CDC e instituições científicas e acadêmicas, como o Clearinghouse Internacional para Vigilância e Pesquisa de Defeitos Congênitos, co-organizou e liderou a Conferência.
DirectResultsFRE:
La conférence a compté 220 participants inscrits, dont 189 étaient présents. Les participants comprenaient des représentants de 25 pays, y compris (par ordre alphabétique) l'Argentine, l'Australie, le Bangladesh, la Bolivie, le Botswana, le Brésil, le Chili, la Colombie, le Costa Rica, l'Angleterre, l'Inde, le Kenya, le Malawi, la Namibie, le Népal, le Nicaragua, le Pakistan, le Paraguay, les Philippines, l'Afrique du Sud, le Sri Lanka, la Suisse, l'Ouganda, les États-Unis et l'Uruguay. 

Au cours de la conférence de quatre jours, il y a eu six sessions plénières et 114 présentations couvrant le dépistage, la surveillance, la prévention, la gestion et les soins, ainsi que l'engagement des parents. Il y avait également 84 affiches présentant une gamme de sujets et de résultats. Un atelier pré-conférence axé sur la surveillance a été dirigé par les CDC et l'OMS avec 32 participants.

Comme prochaine étape de l'ICBD2023, les participants se sont accordés sur l'initiation d'un Appel à l'Action concernant l'intégration, la prévention, le dépistage, le diagnostic et la gestion des malformations congénitales et des handicaps dans les services de santé de routine.
DirectResultsPOR:
A conferência teve 220 participantes registrados, com 189 presentes. Os participantes incluíram representantes de 25 países, incluindo (em ordem alfabética) Argentina, Austrália, Bangladesh, Bolívia, Botsuana, Brasil, Chile, Colômbia, Costa Rica, Inglaterra, Índia, Quênia, Malawi, Namíbia, Nepal, Nicarágua, Paquistão, Paraguai, Filipinas, África do Sul, Sri Lanka, Suíça, Uganda, Estados Unidos e Uruguai. 

Durante os quatro dias de conferência, houve seis sessões plenárias e 114 apresentações abordando triagem, vigilância, prevenção, manejo e cuidados, além do envolvimento dos pais. Também foram apresentados 84 pôsteres com uma variedade de tópicos e descobertas. Um workshop pré-conferência focado em vigilância foi conduzido pelo CDC e pela OMS, com 32 participantes.

Como próximo passo do ICBD2023, os participantes concordaram em iniciar um Chamado à Ação sobre a integração, prevenção, triagem, diagnóstico e manejo de defeitos congênitos e deficiência nos serviços de saúde de rotina.

Title: NSA-2025-39-A_1
Description: In April 2024, UNITE and PAHO jointly contributed to a high-level parliamentary policy dialogue held during the WB & IMF Spring Meetings on ‘Health is Wealth-Parliamentarians as Key Players in Health Financing.’ The activity advanced collaboration to strengthen parliamentary engagement on sustainable health financing and pandemic preparedness and response. PAHO provided technical insights on PPR financing and reinforced parliamentarians’ role in aligning budgets with regional health priorities.
DirectResults:
-Parliamentarians from all over the world gained a better understanding of the societal relevance of health, the economic pressures on healthcare systems, and the need for countries to actively engage at both national and international levels.
-Highlighted parliamentarians’ role in addressing financial constraints and engaging at national and international levels, and identified UNITE’s IFGH as a key connector for national and international budgetary issues and a supporter of 11 sustainable health investments in health.
-Raised awareness among parliamentarians on the importance of an agreement on pandemic prevention and the opportunities for their involvement in its discussions.
History: Joana Perez created a new activity on 12/23/2025 11:59:55 AM.
NSA_x0020_Name: UNITE Parliamentarians Network for Global Health
DescriptionENG: In April 2024, UNITE and PAHO jointly contributed to a high-level parliamentary policy dialogue held during the World Bank and International Monetary Fund Spring Meetings on ‘Health is Wealth - Parliamentarians as Key Players in Health Financing.’ The activity advanced collaboration to strengthen parliamentary engagement on sustainable health financing and pandemic preparedness and response. PAHO provided technical insights on pandemic preparedness and response financing and reinforced parliamentarians’ role in aligning budgets with regional health priorities.
DescriptionSPA: En abril de 2024, UNITE y la OPS contribuyeron conjuntamente a un diálogo político parlamentario de alto nivel celebrado durante las Reuniones de Primavera del BM y el FMI sobre "La salud es riqueza: los parlamentarios como actores clave en la financiación de la salud". La actividad promovió la colaboración para fortalecer el compromiso parlamentario en la financiación sostenible de la salud y la preparación y respuesta ante pandemias. La OPS proporcionó conocimientos técnicos sobre la financiación de la preparación y respuesta (PPR) y reforzó el papel de los parlamentarios en la alineación de los presupuestos con las prioridades de salud regionales.
DirectResultsENG:
- Parliamentarians from all over the world gained a better understanding of the societal relevance of health, the economic pressures on healthcare systems, and the need for countries to actively engage at both national and international levels.
- Highlighted parliamentarians’ role in addressing financial constraints and engaging at national and international levels, and identified UNITE’s IFGH as a key connector for national and international budgetary issues and a supporter of 11 sustainable health investments in health.
- Raised awareness among parliamentarians on the importance of an agreement on pandemic prevention and the opportunities for their involvement in its discussions.
DirectResultsSPA:
- Los parlamentarios de todo el mundo adquirieron una mejor comprensión de la relevancia social de la salud, las presiones económicas sobre los sistemas de salud y la necesidad de que los países se involucren activamente tanto a nivel nacional como internacional.
- Se destacó el papel de los parlamentarios en la atención a las limitaciones financieras y en el compromiso a niveles nacional e internacional, y se identificó el IFGH de UNITE como un conector clave para los problemas presupuestarios nacionales e internacionales y un defensor de 11 inversiones sostenibles en salud.
- Se concienció a los parlamentarios sobre la importancia de un acuerdo sobre la prevención de pandemias y las oportunidades para su participación en las discusiones al respecto.
DescriptionFRE: En avril 2024, l'UNITE et l'OPS ont conjointement contribué à un dialogue politique parlementaire de haut niveau organisé lors des Réunions de Printemps de la Banque Mondiale et du FMI sur le thème « La santé est une richesse - Les parlementaires en tant qu'acteurs clés du financement de la santé. » Cette activité a fait progresser la collaboration pour renforcer l'engagement parlementaire en matière de financement de la santé durable et de préparation et de réponse aux pandémies. L'OPS a fourni des éclairages techniques sur le financement de la préparation et de la réponse aux pandémies et a renforcé le rôle des parlementaires dans l'alignement des budgets sur les priorités de santé régionales.
DescriptionPOR: Em abril de 2024, a UNITE e a OPAS contribuíram conjuntamente para um diálogo político parlamentar de alto nível realizado durante as Reuniões de Primavera do Banco Mundial e do FMI sobre "Saúde é Riqueza - Parlamentares como Jogadores Chave no Financiamento da Saúde". A atividade avançou a colaboração para fortalecer o engajamento parlamentar no financiamento sustentável da saúde e na preparação e resposta a pandemias. A OPAS forneceu insights técnicos sobre o financiamento da preparação e resposta a pandemias e reforçou o papel dos parlamentares na alocação de orçamentos com as prioridades de saúde regionais.
DirectResultsFRE:
- Les parlementaires du monde entier ont acquis une meilleure compréhension de la pertinence sociétale de la santé, des pressions économiques sur les systèmes de santé et de la nécessité pour les pays de s'engager activement à la fois au niveau national et international.
- A mis en avant le rôle des parlementaires dans la gestion des contraintes financières et l'engagement aux niveaux national et international, et a identifié l'IFGH de UNITE comme un connecteur clé pour les questions budgétaires nationales et internationales et un soutien à 11 investissements durables en santé.
- A sensibilisé les parlementaires à l'importance d'un accord sur la prévention des pandémies et aux opportunités de leur implication dans ses discussions.
DirectResultsPOR:
- Parlamentares de todo o mundo adquiriram uma melhor compreensão da relevância social da saúde, das pressões econômicas sobre os sistemas de saúde e da necessidade de os países se envolverem ativamente em níveis nacional e internacional.
- Destacou o papel dos parlamentares na abordagem das restrições financeiras e no engajamento em níveis nacional e internacional, e identificou o IFGH da UNITE como um conector chave para questões orçamentárias nacionais e internacionais e um apoiador de 11 investimentos sustentáveis em saúde.
- Aumentou a conscientização entre os parlamentares sobre a importância de um acordo sobre prevenção de pandemias e as oportunidades para seu envolvimento nas discussões a respeito.

Title: NSA-2025-39-A_2
Description: In March 2024, UNITE, jointly with PAHO, held a regional parliamentary dialogue for LAC under the ‘Let’s Talk Health’ initiative, focused on AMR. The activity was part of structured regional engagements where PAHO provides technical input, regional evidence, and policy guidance to inform parliamentary discussions on priority health issues. PAHO contributed through its AMR Special Program, supporting evidence-based dialogue on legislation, budgeting, and implementation challenges in the Region.
DirectResults:
-As part of its "Let's Talk Health" initiative - a set of dedicated regional sessions on relevant global health priorities, UNITE provided parliamentarians from Latin America and the Caribbean with a platform to share best practices, achievements, and challenges related to AMR legislation, budgeting, and implementation.
- The exchange of experiences—on policy, budgeting, and implementation—offered valuable insights helping inform future legislative proposals.
-PAHO provided the latest evidence-based data on AMR, providing parliamentarians with a regional landscape.
-Strengthened parliamentarians’ capacity to integrate PAHO technical guidance into national AMR legislative and oversight processes.

DescriptionENG: In March 2024, UNITE, in collaboration with PAHO, held a regional parliamentary dialogue for Latin America and the Caribbean under the ‘Let’s Talk Health’ initiative, focusing on antimicrobial resistance (AMR). This activity was part of organized regional engagements where PAHO offers technical input, regional evidence, and policy guidance to inform parliamentary discussions on priority health issues. PAHO contributed through its AMR Special Program, supporting evidence-based dialogue on legislation, budgeting, and implementation challenges in the region.
DescriptionSPA: En marzo de 2024, UNITE, en conjunto con la OPS, llevó a cabo un diálogo parlamentario regional para América Latina y el Caribe bajo la iniciativa 'Hablemos de Salud', centrado en la RAM. La actividad fue parte de compromisos regionales estructurados donde la OPS proporciona insumos técnicos, evidencia regional y orientación política para informar las discusiones parlamentarias sobre temas de salud prioritarios. La OPS contribuyó a través de su Programa Especial de RAM, apoyando un diálogo basado en evidencia sobre legislación, presupuestos y desafíos de implementación en la Región.
DirectResultsENG: As part of its "Let's Talk Health" initiative—a series of dedicated regional sessions focused on relevant global health priorities—UNITE provided parliamentarians from Latin America and the Caribbean with a platform to share best practices, achievements, and challenges related to AMR legislation, budgeting, and implementation. The exchange of experiences regarding policy, budgeting, and implementation offered valuable insights that will help inform future legislative proposals. PAHO provided the latest evidence-based data on AMR, giving parliamentarians a regional perspective. This strengthened the capacity of parliamentarians to integrate PAHO's technical guidance into national AMR legislative and oversight processes.
DirectResultsSPA:
- Como parte de su iniciativa "Hablemos de Salud" - un conjunto de sesiones regionales dedicadas a prioridades globales de salud relevantes, UNITE proporcionó a los parlamentarios de América Latina y el Caribe una plataforma para compartir mejores prácticas, logros y desafíos relacionados con la legislación, el presupuesto y la implementación sobre RAM.
- El intercambio de experiencias—sobre políticas, presupuestos e implementación—ofreció valiosos conocimientos que ayudan a informar futuras propuestas legislativas.
- La OPS proporcionó los últimos datos basados en evidencia sobre RAM, brindando a los parlamentarios un panorama regional.
- Se fortaleció la capacidad de los parlamentarios para integrar la orientación técnica de la OPS en los procesos legislativos y de supervisión nacionales sobre RAM.
DescriptionFRE: En mars 2024, UNITE, en collaboration avec l'OPS, a organisé un dialogue parlementaire régional pour l'Amérique Latine et les Caraïbes dans le cadre de l'initiative « Parlons Santé », axé sur la RAM. Cette activité faisait partie d'engagements régionaux structurés où l'OPS fournit des contributions techniques, des preuves régionales et des orientations politiques pour éclairer les discussions parlementaires sur les questions de santé prioritaires. L'OPS a contribué par le biais de son Programme spécial sur la RAM, soutenant un dialogue fondé sur des preuves concernant la législation, le budget et les défis de mise en œuvre dans la région.
DescriptionPOR: Em março de 2024, a UNITE, em conjunto com a OPAS, realizou um diálogo parlamentar regional para a América Latina e o Caribe (ALC) sob a iniciativa "Vamos Falar sobre Saúde", com foco na resistência antimicrobiana (RAM). A atividade fez parte de compromissos regionais estruturados, onde a OPAS fornece insumos técnicos, evidências regionais e orientações políticas para informar as discussões parlamentares sobre questões prioritárias de saúde. A OPAS contribuiu por meio de seu Programa Especial de RAM, apoiando o diálogo baseado em evidências sobre legislação, orçamento e desafios de implementação na Região.
DirectResultsFRE:
- Dans le cadre de son initiative "Parlons Santé" - un ensemble de sessions régionales dédiées aux priorités mondiales de santé, UNITE a offert aux parlementaires d'Amérique latine et des Caraïbes une plateforme pour partager les meilleures pratiques, les réalisations et les défis liés à la législation sur la RAM, au budget et à la mise en œuvre.
- L'échange d'expériences - sur les politiques, le budget et la mise en œuvre - a offert des perspectives précieuses pour éclairer les futures propositions législatives.
- L'OPS a fourni les dernières données probantes sur la RAM, offrant aux parlementaires un aperçu régional.
- A renforcé la capacité des parlementaires à intégrer les orientations techniques de l'OPS dans les processus législatifs et de surveillance nationaux sur la RAM.
DirectResultsPOR:
- Como parte de sua iniciativa "Vamos Falar sobre Saúde" - um conjunto de sessões regionais dedicadas a prioridades globais de saúde relevantes, a UNITE ofereceu aos parlamentares da América Latina e do Caribe uma plataforma para compartilhar melhores práticas, conquistas e desafios relacionados à legislação, orçamento e implementação sobre AMR.
- A troca de experiências - sobre políticas, orçamentos e implementação - ofereceu insights valiosos que ajudaram a informar propostas legislativas futuras.
- A OPAS forneceu os dados mais recentes baseados em evidências sobre AMR, apresentando aos parlamentares um panorama regional.
- Fortaleceu a capacidade dos parlamentares de integrar as orientações técnicas da OPAS nos processos legislativos e de supervisão nacional sobre AMR.

Title: NSA-2025-39-A_3
Description: In September 2023, UNITE PAHO and the "Observatorio de Salud of Universidad de Buenos Aires" co-hosted a roundtable titled "The Future of the Parliamentary Agenda on Digital Health".The event aimed to foster dialogue on the digital transformation of health and explore strategies for building effective digital health governance mechanisms in Argentina.This roundtable formed part of UNITE’s and PAHO’s ongoing collaboration on digital health governance and health-related legislation in the Americas
DirectResults:
-PAHO, through the participation of Dr. Marcelo D’ Agostino, Unit Chief for Information Systems and Digital Health, provided key insights and evidence-based data on the regional digital health landscape.
-The roundtable provided a platform for parliamentarians and experts to engage in dialogue on digital health transformation models in Argentina, with a focus on advancing Universal Health Coverage (UHC).
- Participants discussed the potential creation of governance parameters to ensure security, privacy, interoperability, ethical use of data, equal access, and inclusion.
- Identified the main governance challenges and opportunities in Argentina and Latin America, and gathered expert suggestions for designing effective regulatory structures.
- Incorporated lessons learned and best practices into a set of recommendations for future elected officials.

NSA_x0020_Name: UNITE Parliamentarians Network for Global Health
DescriptionENG: In September 2023, UNITE PAHO and the "Health Observatory of the University of Buenos Aires" co-hosted a roundtable titled "The Future of the Parliamentary Agenda on Digital Health." The event aimed to foster dialogue on the digital transformation of health and explore strategies for building effective digital health governance mechanisms in Argentina. This roundtable was part of UNITE’s and PAHO’s ongoing collaboration on digital health governance and health-related legislation in the Americas.
DescriptionSPA: En septiembre de 2023, UNITE PAHO y el "Observatorio de Salud de la Universidad de Buenos Aires" coorganizaron una mesa redonda titulada "El Futuro de la Agenda Parlamentaria sobre Salud Digital". El evento tuvo como objetivo fomentar el diálogo sobre la transformación digital de la salud y explorar estrategias para construir mecanismos de gobernanza de salud digital efectivos en Argentina. Esta mesa redonda formó parte de la colaboración continua de UNITE y PAHO en la gobernanza de la salud digital y la legislación relacionada con la salud en las Américas.
DirectResultsENG:
- PAHO, through the participation of Dr. Marcelo D’Agostino, Unit Chief for Information Systems and Digital Health, provided key insights and evidence-based data on the regional digital health landscape.
- The roundtable provided a platform for parliamentarians and experts to engage in dialogue on digital health transformation models in Argentina, with a focus on advancing Universal Health Coverage (UHC).
- Participants discussed the potential creation of governance parameters to ensure security, privacy, interoperability, ethical use of data, equal access, and inclusion.
- Identified the main governance challenges and opportunities in Argentina and Latin America, and gathered expert suggestions for designing effective regulatory structures.
- Incorporated lessons learned and best practices into a set of recommendations for future elected officials.
DirectResultsSPA:
- La OPS, a través de la participación del Dr. Marcelo D’ Agostino, Jefe de la Unidad de Sistemas de Información y Salud Digital, proporcionó información clave y datos basados en evidencia sobre el panorama de la salud digital en la región.
- La mesa redonda ofreció una plataforma para que parlamentarios y expertos participaran en un diálogo sobre modelos de transformación de la salud digital en Argentina, con un enfoque en avanzar hacia la Cobertura Universal de Salud (CUS).
- Los participantes discutieron la posible creación de parámetros de gobernanza para garantizar la seguridad, la privacidad, la interoperabilidad, el uso ético de los datos, el acceso equitativo y la inclusión.
- Se identificaron los principales desafíos y oportunidades de gobernanza en Argentina y América Latina, y se recopilaron sugerencias de expertos para diseñar estructuras regulatorias efectivas.
- Se incorporaron lecciones aprendidas y mejores prácticas en un conjunto de recomendaciones para futuros funcionarios electos.
DescriptionFRE: En septembre 2023, UNITE PAHO et l'« Observatorio de Salud de l'Université de Buenos Aires » ont co-organisé une table ronde intitulée « L'avenir de l'agenda parlementaire sur la santé numérique ». L'événement visait à favoriser le dialogue sur la transformation numérique de la santé et à explorer des stratégies pour établir des mécanismes de gouvernance de la santé numérique efficaces en Argentine. Cette table ronde faisait partie de la collaboration continue entre UNITE et PAHO sur la gouvernance de la santé numérique et la législation liée à la santé dans les Amériques.
DescriptionPOR: Em setembro de 2023, a UNITE PAHO e o "Observatório de Saúde da Universidade de Buenos Aires" co-organizaram uma mesa-redonda intitulada "O Futuro da Agenda Parlamentar sobre Saúde Digital". O evento teve como objetivo promover o diálogo sobre a transformação digital da saúde e explorar estratégias para construir mecanismos eficazes de governança em saúde digital na Argentina. Esta mesa-redonda fez parte da colaboração contínua da UNITE e da PAHO em governança de saúde digital e legislação relacionada à saúde nas Américas.
DirectResultsFRE:
- L'OPS, par la participation du Dr. Marcelo D’Agostino, Chef de l'Unité des Systèmes d'Information et de la Santé Numérique, a fourni des informations clés et des données basées sur des preuves concernant le paysage de la santé numérique dans la région.
- La table ronde a offert une plateforme aux parlementaires et aux experts pour dialoguer sur les modèles de transformation de la santé numérique en Argentine, en mettant l'accent sur l'avancement de la Couverture Sanitaire Universelle (CSU).
- Les participants ont discuté de la création potentielle de paramètres de gouvernance pour garantir la sécurité, la confidentialité, l'interopérabilité, l'utilisation éthique des données, l'accès équitable et l'inclusion.
- Ils ont identifié les principaux défis et opportunités de gouvernance en Argentine et en Amérique latine, et ont recueilli des suggestions d'experts pour concevoir des structures réglementaires efficaces.
- Ils ont intégré les leçons apprises et les meilleures pratiques dans un ensemble de recommandations pour les futurs élus.
DirectResultsPOR:
- A OPAS, por meio da participação do Dr. Marcelo D’Agostino, Chefe da Unidade de Sistemas de Informação e Saúde Digital, forneceu insights importantes e dados baseados em evidências sobre o panorama da saúde digital na região.
- A mesa-redonda ofereceu uma plataforma para que parlamentares e especialistas dialogassem sobre modelos de transformação da saúde digital na Argentina, com foco na promoção da Cobertura Universal de Saúde (CUS).
- Os participantes discutiram a potencial criação de parâmetros de governança para garantir segurança, privacidade, interoperabilidade, uso ético de dados, acesso igualitário e inclusão.
- Identificaram os principais desafios e oportunidades de governança na Argentina e na América Latina, e reuniram sugestões de especialistas para a elaboração de estruturas regulatórias eficazes.
- Incorporaram lições aprendidas e melhores práticas em um conjunto de recomendações para futuros representantes eleitos.

Title: NSA-2025-39-A_4
Description: During the World Health Summit 2022, UNITE and WHO held an official session on “The Role of Parliamentarians in the Development of a Global Pandemic Treaty.” PAHO supported the participation of four parliamentarians from the Americas, bringing regional perspectives to discussions. PAHO’s involvement extended beyond facilitating participation, contributing to the integration of regional parliamentary perspectives from the Americas into global discussions on the pandemic agreement.
DirectResults:
-Raised parliamentarians’ awareness of the importance of a global pandemic agreement.
- Highlighted opportunities for parliamentarians to engage in its discussions.

DescriptionPOR: Durante a Cúpula Mundial da Saúde 2022, a UNITE e a OMS realizaram uma sessão oficial sobre "O Papel dos Parlamentares no Desenvolvimento de um Tratado Global sobre Pandemias". A OPAS apoiou a participação de quatro parlamentares das Américas, trazendo perspectivas regionais para as discussões. O envolvimento da OPAS foi além de facilitar a participação, contribuindo para a integração das perspectivas parlamentares regionais das Américas nas discussões globais sobre o acordo pandêmico.
DirectResultsFRE:
- Sensibilisé les parlementaires à l'importance d'un accord mondial sur les pandémies.
- Mis en avant les opportunités pour les parlementaires de participer à ses discussions.
DirectResultsPOR:
- Aumentou a conscientização dos parlamentares sobre a importância de um acordo global para pandemias.
- Destacou oportunidades para os parlamentares se envolverem em suas discussões.

Title: NSA-2025-39-A_5
Description: At the UNITE Global Summit 2022, UNITE, WHO, and UHC2030 hosted a Regional Dialogue on UHC, engaging parliamentarians in discussions on key political drivers, regional progress, and mobilizing domestic resources to advance UHC. The dialogue promoted best practice sharing and raising awareness among parliamentarians on achieving UHC in every country.
DirectResults:
-The Summit concluded with the signing of a Declaration of Intent by the attending parliamentarians, which included a strong delegation of members from UNITE's Latin America and the Caribbean Chapter. 
-The declaration endorsed a Health in All Policies approach and emphasized the importance of involving parliamentarians in negotiations for an international Pandemic Agreement.
-Furthermore, the declaration encouraged multilateralism and international cooperation to strengthen health systems and improve health financing—critical steps toward advancing sustainable health policies and achieving the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
